preamble: The development of a retroflex stop [ʈ] into a retroflex affricate [ʈʂ]. This process, known as affrication, involves the slow release of the stop closure, creating a period of audible friction.

examples.note: Dialectal variation where retroflex stops are realized as affricates.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
